articles.title: Lakers Edge Rockets 101-94 to Take Series Lead
articles.original_title: Lakers 101-94 Rockets (Apr 21, 2026) Game Recap - ESPN
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers defeated the Houston Rockets 101-94 on April 21, 2026, improving their postseason standing. LeBron James led the scoring while the team’s defense limited Houston’s offensive output.
key_points: Final score: Lakers 101, Rockets 94.
LeBron James was the leading scorer for Los Angeles.
Lakers’ defense held the Rockets to under 100 points for the second straight game.

articles.title: Lakers’ Defense Steps Up as Rockets Struggle
articles.original_title: Lakers defense rises to the challenge Kevin Durant, Rockets gave them
articles.summary: After relying on offense earlier in the season, the Lakers have shifted to a defense‑first approach, helping them win playoff games against the Rockets. Injuries to Luka Dončić and Austin Reaves forced adjustments that emphasized perimeter and interior pressure.
key_points: Defensive intensity has become the primary factor in recent Lakers victories.
Injuries to key players prompted a strategic shift from offense to defense.
The Rockets have been unable to score efficiently against the Lakers’ new defensive schemes.

articles.title: Lakers Win Game 2 with New ‘Big Three’ Lineup
articles.original_title: Lakers beat Rockets in Game 2 with a new Big Three - Los Angeles Times
articles.summary: Los Angeles secured a Game 2 victory over Houston by deploying a revamped ‘Big Three’ rotation, which proved effective against the Rockets’ frontcourt. The new configuration gave the Lakers a 2‑0 series advantage.
key_points: A new ‘Big Three’ lineup was introduced in Game 2.
The rotation helped the Lakers dominate the paint and control the tempo.
The win gave Los Angeles a 2‑0 lead in the series.

articles.title: Player Grades: Lakers vs. Rockets
articles.original_title: Player Grades: Lakers vs. Rockets
articles.summary: An analysis of individual performances highlighted the contributions of role players like Marcus Smart and Luke Kennard, who stepped up in the series despite not reaching historic Lakers standards. Their efforts complemented the star power of LeBron James and Anthony Davis.
key_points: Marcus Smart and Luke Kennard received solid grades for their play.
Role players provided crucial support in key moments of the series.
The article compares current contributors to past Lakers legends.

articles.title: LeBron Defies Age as Lakers Stifle Kevin Durant, Take 2‑0 Lead
articles.original_title: 4 takeaways: LeBron James defies age and Lakers limit Kevin Durant to take 2-0 lead - NBA
articles.summary: LeBron James showcased his enduring skill by limiting Kevin Durant’s impact in Game 2, helping the Lakers move up 2‑0 against the Rockets. The performance underscored Los Angeles’ balanced attack and defensive focus.
key_points: LeBron James effectively contained Kevin Durant, who returned for Houston.
Lakers now lead the series 2‑0.
The win highlighted the team’s ability to blend veteran leadership with defensive execution.

articles.title: Lakers Edge Rockets 101-94 in Game 2
articles.original_title: 3 takeaways from Lakers’ Game 2 win vs. Rockets
articles.summary: The Lakers held off a physical Rockets team to win Game 2, 101-94, taking a 2-0 series lead. Kevin Durant returned for the Rockets, but Los Angeles matched their intensity in the closing minutes.
key_points: Lakers secured a 2-0 advantage in the series with a 101-94 victory.
Kevin Durant made his first appearance for the Rockets, but the Lakers limited his impact.
Los Angeles matched the Rockets' physical play down the stretch to close out the game.

articles.title: LeBron James Shines as Wembanyama Leaves Playoffs with Concussion
articles.original_title: NBA play-offs: LeBron James shines for Los Angeles Lakers while Victor Wembanyama concussed - BBC
articles.summary: LeBron James delivered a standout performance for the Lakers in the playoffs while Houston’s rookie Victor Wembanyama suffered a concussion. The incident highlighted the physical nature of the series and underscored James' leadership on the court.
key_points: LeBron posted a strong statistical line, reinforcing his role as the Lakers' primary scorer.
Houston's top prospect Victor Wembanyama was diagnosed with a concussion during the game.
The injury raised concerns about Wembanyama's availability for the remainder of the series.

articles.title: Marcus Smart’s Take on LeBron James Resonates with Lakers Fans
articles.original_title: Marcus Smart says exactly what Lakers fans know about LeBron James - Lake Show Life
articles.summary: In a recent interview, Marcus Smart articulated observations about LeBron James that align with long‑time Lakers supporters. Smart highlighted James' work ethic, leadership, and impact on team culture.
key_points: Smart praised James' dedication and willingness to make teammates better.
He noted James' ability to elevate the Lakers in clutch moments.
The comments echoed common fan sentiments about James' influence on and off the court.

articles.title: LeBron Leads Lakers to Game 2 Win, Dampening Durant’s Return

Kevin Durant’s return for the Rockets did not shift the game's momentum.
The victory put the Lakers in a strong position heading into Game 3.

articles.title: Wemby concussed on hard fall in Spurs' G2 loss
articles.original_title: Wemby concussed on hard fall in Spurs' G2 loss
articles.summary: Spurs star Victor Wembanyama sustained a concussion on a hard fall during the second quarter of Tuesday night's Game 2 playoff loss to the Portland Trail Blazers.

articles.title: Edgecombe makes history with 30-10 to stun C's
articles.original_title: Edgecombe makes history with 30-10 to stun C's
articles.summary: VJ Edgecombe had 30 points and 10 rebounds while playing through pain after taking a hard fall on his back early in the game, Tyrese Maxey scored 29 points and the 76ers beat the Celtics 111-97 on Tuesday night to tie their series at one game apiece.

articles.title: Smart's defense on KD helps Lakers to 2-0 lead
articles.original_title: Smart's defense on KD helps Lakers to 2-0 lead
articles.summary: The Rockets had Kevin Durant back for Game 2 of their first-round matchup Tuesday. But the Lakers had Marcus Smart guard him in their 101-94 win to take a 2-0 series lead.

articles.title: Scoot plays his best game of season, fuels Blazers
articles.original_title: Scoot plays his best game of season, fuels Blazers
articles.summary: After struggling to stay healthy in 2025-26, Scoot Henderson broke out with a season-high 31 points to lead the Trail Blazers past the Spurs on Tuesday night and even their first-round playoff series at one game apiece.

articles.title: Donovan leaves Bulls amid sweeping changes
articles.original_title: Donovan leaves Bulls amid sweeping changes
articles.summary: Billy Donovan has decided to step down as head coach of the Bulls after six seasons.

articles.title: Splitter Stays Focused on Basketball Amid Portland Cost‑Cutting Rumors
articles.original_title: Splitter on Dundon reports: 'Trying to be a pro'
articles.summary: Interim Portland Trail Blazers coach Tiago Splitter addressed media questions about owner Tom Dundon's reported cost‑cutting measures. He said his priority is the on‑court product and that he is "trying to be a pro" by keeping the team’s focus on basketball.
key_points: Splitter declined to discuss financial details, emphasizing coaching responsibilities.
He noted the importance of maintaining player development despite any budget constraints.
The comment came as speculation grew about the franchise’s financial direction under Dundon.

articles.title: Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander Wins Clutch Player of the Year
articles.original_title: Gilgeous-Alexander wins Clutch Player of the Year
articles.summary: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander was named the NBA’s Clutch Player of the Year, adding to his recent MVP award. The honor recognizes his performance in high‑pressure moments throughout the season.
key_points: Gilgeous‑Alexander averaged a career‑high in points while delivering decisive plays in the fourth quarter.
The award highlights his consistency in close games and late‑game situations.
He joins a select group of players who have earned both MVP and Clutch Player honors in the same season.

articles.title: Jalen Duren Vows More Aggression After Limited Shots in Game 1
articles.original_title: Duren vows more aggression after 4 FGAs in G1
articles.summary: After being held to just four field‑goal attempts in Game 1, Orlando Magic rookie Jalen Duren promised a more aggressive approach in Game 2. The Magic’s defensive scheme limited his touches, prompting the call‑up.
key_points: Duren’s low shot volume was a result of the Magic’s “suffocating” defense on the opposing first‑time All‑Star.
He pledged to attack the rim and seek more scoring opportunities in the upcoming game.
The statement underscores the Magic’s intent to adjust offensive flow while maintaining defensive intensity.

articles.title: Celebrity Sightings Heat Up Courtside During NBA Playoffs
articles.original_title: 📸 Celebrity sightings at NBA playoffs games
articles.summary: A variety of high‑profile celebrities have been spotted courtside at NBA playoff games, adding star power to the already electric atmosphere. Their presence reflects the growing cultural crossover between basketball and entertainment.
key_points: Celebrities were seen at multiple venues, often sitting with former players or team executives.
Social media posts highlighted fashion choices and reactions to key moments on the floor.
The trend underscores the NBA’s appeal beyond traditional sports audiences.

articles.title: Lakers Take 2‑0 Lead Over Rockets as Role Players Shine
articles.original_title: LeBron James outduels Kevin Durant, but it's Marcus Smart and Luke Kennard that have Lakers up 2-0 on Rockets
articles.summary: Los Angeles Lakers secured a 2‑0 series advantage against the Houston Rockets, with LeBron James outdueling Kevin Durant in the paint. Marcus Smart and Luke Kennard provided crucial scoring, while Houston’s offense struggled to find rhythm.
key_points: LeBron’s defensive effort limited Durant’s impact, contributing to the Lakers’ early lead.
Smart and Kennard combined for key baskets that kept the Lakers ahead throughout the game.
The Rockets displayed offensive stagnation, prompting concerns about adjustments for the remainder of the series.

articles.title: LeBron James Defies Age, Holds Lakers' Lead as Injured Luka Doncic Watches
articles.original_title: Age-defying LeBron James holds the line for Lakers as injured Luka Doncic looks on - MARCA
articles.summary: Veteran forward LeBron James continued to anchor the Los Angeles Lakers with a strong performance, keeping the team competitive while Dallas Mavericks star Luka Doncic was sidelined with injury. The article highlights James' durability and leadership despite being in the later stage of his career.
key_points: LeBron James delivered a solid game, showcasing his continued effectiveness at 38 years old.
Luka Doncic missed the matchup due to injury, limiting the Mavericks' offensive firepower.
James' performance underscores his role as the Lakers' primary scorer and defensive presence.

articles.title: LeBron Scores 28, Lakers Take 2‑0 Edge Over Rockets in Playoffs
articles.original_title: LeBron James nets 28, Lakers grab 2-0 lead on Rockets in NBA Playoff series - Al Jazeera
articles.summary: In Game 2 of the first‑round series, LeBron James poured in 28 points to help Los Angeles secure a 2‑0 lead against the Houston Rockets. The win puts the Lakers in a commanding position as the series shifts to Houston for Game 3.
key_points: LeBron James finished with 28 points, leading all scorers in the game.
The Lakers now lead the series 2‑0, putting pressure on the Rockets.
Houston struggled offensively, allowing the Lakers to control the tempo.

articles.title: Iman Shumpert Says LeBron Playing Final Season With Cavs Would Be 'Coolest Story'
articles.original_title: Iman Shumpert Says LeBron Playing Final Season With Cavs Would Be 'Coolest Story' - TMZ
articles.summary: Former NBA player Iman Shumpert commented that seeing LeBron James finish his career with the Cleveland Cavaliers would be an intriguing narrative. Shumpert's remarks were made in a light‑hearted interview and do not reflect any confirmed plans.
key_points: Shumpert speculated that a final season with the Cavs would create a compelling storyline.
No official indications that LeBron is considering a move to Cleveland.
The comment reflects broader fan interest in potential late‑career moves for James.

articles.title: LeBron James Becomes Oldest Player to Lead Team in Playoff Scoring
articles.original_title: LeBron James becomes oldest player to lead a team in scoring in the playoffs - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: Yahoo Sports reported that LeBron James set a new NBA record by becoming the oldest player to lead his team in points per game during a playoff series. The milestone came as he continued to dominate for the Lakers in the first round.
key_points: James, at 38, topped the Lakers in scoring average for the playoff series.
The record surpasses the previous oldest scorer‑leader in postseason history.
His performance reinforces his status as a premier playoff performer despite age.

articles.title: LeBron Leads Lakers to 2‑0 Series Edge Over Rockets
articles.original_title: LeBron James turns back the clock as Lakers top Rockets for 2-0 series lead - Orange County Register
articles.summary: LeBron James scored 28 points as the Los Angeles Lakers defeated the Houston Rockets 101‑94, taking a 2‑0 lead in the first‑round series. The victory came despite the Lakers missing several key players, highlighting James' ability to dominate in a short‑handed lineup.
key_points: LeBron posted 28 points, anchoring the Lakers' offense.
Lakers won Game 2 by a seven‑point margin, 101‑94.
The win gave LA a 2‑0 series lead despite injury setbacks.

articles.title: Short‑Handed Lakers Hold Off Rockets for Second Straight Win

key_points: LeBron's 28 points came while the Lakers were missing several starters.
Houston's Kevin Durant was limited by injuries and could not match his usual output.
The win extended the Lakers' series lead to 2‑0.
